ref: The Lord told Noah: Take your whole family with you into the boat, because you are the only one on this earth who pleases me.
text: Korowii ŋɔ Nowa be ŋɔ, “He ne he bii ne he bii haahna bwa ka jo daka la bini. N nao kɛŋɔ harɛ nɔ ta, he ɡboo ɛ nɛn he wa aa toɡra.
translated: Nowa said, “He will not be able to do anything because he is too weak. He will not be able to fight back, he will just be beaten.”

ref: She also realized that Shelah was now a grown man, but she had not been allowed to marry him. So she decided to dress in something other than her widow's clothes and to cover her face with a veil. After this, she sat outside the town of Enaim on the road to Timnah.
text: Asɛ o foo o laala waa doa so doŋa ta la bil, dɛ kpɔ kon waa chaahna bɔle nyu, dɛ ka so bwɛɛ Enaem doonyoa, Temna ŋwɛnɛ ta. Dɛkalkɛ dɛ o nao kɛŋɔ Seela kpeɡo, asɛ ba ɡe ta on kpɔe tɛo ŋɔ o yalɔ.
translated: The one who is wise, he will speak the truth, and he will not hide it. He will speak the truth, and he will not be afraid. He will speak the truth, and he will not be ashamed. He will speak the truth, and he will not be silent. He will speak the truth, and he will not be afraid of death.

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: kɛ o vɛh Keen ne kwaa waa kpɔa ba ɛ saraha tɛo la. Belaa Keen baah di asɛ o ŋweh.
translated: He went to the farm and worked hard. He came back home tired.

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Awola jwaana la ne la, ba tɛo sen o nyɔ, asɛ o ne o binomoa la chɔ. Waa dɛɛra nomanta, o ta o ne o bihaah la chɔe ne o ira saŋ bɛlbwa nae.”
translated: The old man said, "I have seen many things, but I have never seen anything like this. This is truly a strange and wonderful thing."

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Kɛ Korowii koh Sarai waa dɛ ɡel weechɔɡa ane ba le kooro la ne o dem tena bwa nɛ.
translated: The people of Korowii and Sarai are very close to each other, they are like one family.
